title: Woman, 25, dead, driver and 3 children hospitalized in crash on I-495 in Andover
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: A 25-year-old woman from Lawrence died in a minivan crash on I-495 North in Andover.
The minivan driver was hospitalized with serious injuries; three children had unknown injuries.
The crash with another vehicle left the minivan engulfed in flames, under investigation by State Police.

title: 3 longtime tenants to close or move after Alpha Management buys prominent Allston commercial building
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Alpha Management's $12.25 million purchase of 149-175 Harvard Ave. leads to tenant changes in Allston.
The Pet Shop, Ritual Arts, and Regeneration Tattoo are closing or moving due to unaffordable rent increases.
Alpha Management plans renovations, claiming the building lacked investment and charged below-market rents for years.

title: Paul Pierce, Kevin Garnett to introduce Doc Rivers at Hall of Fame ceremony
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Doc Rivers will be introduced by Paul Pierce and Kevin Garnett at the Basketball Hall of Fame ceremony.
Rivers ranks sixth in all-time NBA coaching wins with 1,194 victories, second among Celtics coaches after Red Auerbach.
The Hall of Fame class includes Elena Delle Donne, Candace Parker, and Amar'e Stoudemire; the ceremony is August 15.

title: Jayson Tatum says it’s ‘weird’ that Jaylen Brown is no longer a Celtic
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jayson Tatum described it as 'weird' that Jaylen Brown, his long-time teammate, is now a Philadelphia 76er.
The Celtics traded Brown to the 76ers for Paul George, two first-round picks, and two second-round picks.
Fans reacted emotionally online, with a viral video of a young fan's tearful response to Brown's trade.

title: How many first-round picks would Drake Maye be worth?
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: ESPN's Bill Barnwell values Patriots QB Drake Maye at six first-round draft picks.
Drake Maye stands alongside Josh Allen and Patrick Mahomes in ESPN's top tier of valuable NFL players.
Maye has reached a Super Bowl, been an MVP runner-up, and earned two Pro Bowl selections at age 23.

title: Furnished island home on Lake Winnipesaukee — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: A furnished island home in Gilford, N.H. with Lake Winnipesaukee views is listed for $889,500.
The 1,037-square-foot cottage, built in 1952, has 3 bedrooms, 1 bathroom, and offers rental potential.
Property taxes for the Lake Winnipesaukee home are $8,027 annually as of 2025.

title: ‘The Odyssey’ review: Christopher Nolan defies the odds (and the gods) with a spectacular retelling of Homer’s epic
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Christopher Nolan's 'The Odyssey' presents a human-centric retelling of Homer’s epic, with Matt Damon playing Odysseus.
The film features a non-linear narrative and focuses on Odysseus' personal struggles over interactions with gods.
'The Odyssey' highlights Nolan's cinematic style, showcasing stunning practical effects and a contemporary interpretation of the classic tale.

title: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle just got married and celebrated at this North End restaurant
author: John Waller
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Payton Tolle married his partner Devin during the All-Star break.
The couple celebrated their marriage at Arya Trattoria in Boston's North End.
Tolle, an Oklahoma native, was drafted by the Red Sox in 2024 and has a 3.59 ERA.

title: Patriots 2026 position preview: Defensive Line edition
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Milton Williams and Christian Barmore lead the Patriots' strong interior defensive line, producing 86 pressures last season.
Patriots' defensive line allowed 101.7 rushing yards per game, ranking sixth in the league for the 2025 season.
Despite losing Khyiris Tonga, the Patriots maintain depth, but have less strength on the defensive edge.

title: 28-year-old killed in Mass. skydiving accident was a Northeastern grad
author: John Waller
bullets: Mani Chandra Teja Gaddam, 28, died in a skydiving accident in Orange, MA due to a sudden wind gust.
The accident occurred at Jumptown Skydiving where a dust devil interfered with Gaddam's parachute, leading to his fall.
Gaddam, a Northeastern graduate and software developer, had recently obtained his A License in skydiving.
